body:
## Summary
- Add sub-orchestrator marker helpers and a child issue creation CLI.
- Allow agent-mode parent issue orchestration to create or reuse child issues and dispatch normal child `/orchestrate` runs.
- Report terminal child issue/PR results back to the parent issue, update child state, and resume the parent planner.
- Update orchestrator prompt/docs and add regression tests for child creation, reuse, and terminal report-back.

## Verification
- `npm test`

Closes #51

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #66 for issue #51; the next useful automated step is review.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777562914055 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNzM2NDI0NzQ6aW1wbGVtZW50OjUxOnJldmlldzo2Njoz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 81 | PARTIAL | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Terminal child stops post/update visible parent progress comments and child state before parent resume ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L306-L363)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `/orchestrate` delegated decisions still pass through route authorization before dispatch ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L470-L494)); resumed parent dispatch preserves requester/access context ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L346-L363)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 4/7 | Planner output uses explicit fields and validates planner `base_branch` vs `base_pr` ([handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L145-L166), [handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L374-L376)), but inherited base inputs can still combine with planner base inputs at dispatch. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | partial | 5/8 | Marker formatting/parsing is centralized in `sub-orchestration.ts`, but child issue creation logic is duplicated between the new standalone CLI and `orchestrate-handoff.ts` instead of reusing the helper path. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 4/7 | Terminal callback failures thrown from the top-level report path are warned, but GitHub read failures inside `fetchIssue`/`readPrBody` are swallowed into `null`/empty results, making some report-back failures silent ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L148-L168), [orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7afc26aabb59b8a17318e92ab7f5eba906a2c1e5/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L278-L303)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updated both workflow architecture and orchestrator technical docs for parent/child orchestration, existing issue reuse, terminal callback, and parent resume behavior. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are confined to orchestrator handoff logic, sub-orchestration helpers, prompt/docs, workflow preflight wiring, and focused tests. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | partial | 5/7 | The model is conceptually simpler than lane threading, but the implementation carries duplicated child-issue create/annotate logic and broad handoff CLI growth. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric alignment is the design shift toward GitHub issue metadata, visible parent comments, normal child `/orchestrate` dispatch, docs updates, and focused regression tests for creation, reuse, PR terminal resolution, and parent resume.
- The main deductions are validation/observability edges in stateful orchestration code: mixed inherited/planner base inputs, accepting existing child issues without checking marker ownership/state, and swallowed GitHub read failures during terminal callback.
- I attempted `npm test`; it did not complete because TypeScript could not write generated `.agent/dist/*` files in this sandbox (`EPERM`).

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `ensureSubOrchestrationIssue` only rejects `decision.baseBranch && decision.basePr`, but then merges planner and inherited values with `decision.baseBranch || baseBranch` and `decision.basePr || basePr` for both the child issue body and child orchestrator dispatch. A parent run with `BASE_BRANCH` plus a planner-selected `base_pr`, or vice versa, can dispatch the child with both base inputs despite the explicit-input contract and prompt rule. This is tied to `Prefer explicit structured inputs`.

- **WARNING:** Reusing an existing child issue accepts any issue that already has any `sepo-sub-orchestrator` marker; it does not verify that the marker parent, stage, or running state matches the requested parent/stage before dispatching that issue as the child. That can route a child run to stale or wrong parent metadata. This is tied to `Prefer explicit structured inputs` and `Surface stateful failures`.

- **INFO:** The user requested parent/sub-issue linking “if not too hard.” The PR records `Parent issue: #...` and hidden markers, but does not add GitHub sub-issue/parent links. That is acceptable as a follow-up based on the request wording, but it is not fully implemented here.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #66 implements child issue sub-orchestrators for parent `/orchestrate` runs.
- It adds marker helpers, child issue creation/reuse, terminal child report-back to the parent, and parent orchestrator resume dispatch.
- The PR also updates orchestrator docs/prompts and adds regression coverage for child creation, reuse, and terminal PR result handling.
- This supports issue #51 by moving multi-stage orchestration state into GitHub issue metadata instead of trying to thread multiple concurrent lanes through one workflow run.

## Review
- The design direction is strong, but reviewers found retry-safety and state-validation gaps in the new orchestration state machine.
- The terminal callback can permanently lose a parent resume after a partial failure, so this should not ship as-is.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Terminal callback is not retry-safe | BLOCKING | The child marker is changed to a terminal state before the parent orchestrator dispatch succeeds. A dispatch failure after the child body update can cause future retries to skip parent resume entirely. |
| Existing child issue reuse does not validate marker ownership | BLOCKING | Explicit child issue reuse accepts any existing `sepo-sub-orchestrator` marker without verifying parent, stage, or reusable state. This can dispatch work to the wrong issue or report completion to the wrong parent. |
| Child base inputs can merge into an invalid combination | WARNING | `ensureSubOrchestrationIssue` validates only planner-provided `base_branch`/`base_pr`, then merges inherited values later. A parent run can still dispatch a child with both base inputs set. |
| Duplicate child lookup may be unreliable | WARNING | The duplicate lookup relies on GitHub issue search matching raw marker tokens containing colon syntax. If search interprets those as qualifiers, duplicate child issues may be created. |
| GitHub native sub-issue linking is not implemented | INFO | The PR records parent/child metadata in issue bodies and hidden markers, but does not create GitHub parent/sub-issue links. Reviewers considered this acceptable follow-up scope because the request phrased it as optional. |

## Progress
- The PR already includes docs and focused tests for the main happy paths.
- A rubrics review has already called out the base-input merge and existing-child marker validation issues.
- I posted one new inline comment for the retry-safety blocker at `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:341`.
- Reviewers attempted tests, but full `npm test` did not complete in their environments due setup/sandbox issues rather than a confirmed test failure.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Terminal callback is not retry-safe</summary>

**Cause:** `reportTerminalToParent()` writes the parent progress comment and updates the child marker to `done` / `blocked` / `failed` before calling `dispatchWorkflow()` to resume the parent orchestrator.

**Candidate solutions:** Dispatch the parent before marking the child terminal, or introduce a separate callback/resume state so reruns can finish the parent dispatch after a partial failure.

**Comments:** This is the highest-risk issue because the caller catches the error and exits cleanly, while later reruns return early when the marker is no longer `state:running`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Existing child issue reuse does not validate marker ownership</summary>

**Cause:** When `decision.childIssueNumber` is provided, the code checks only whether some sub-orchestrator marker exists, not whether it matches the requested parent issue, normalized stage, and reusable state.

**Candidate solutions:** Parse the existing marker and require matching `parent`, matching normalized `stage`, and an allowed state such as `running`; otherwise fail clearly instead of dispatching.

**Comments:** Both reviewers identified this risk, though one classified it as blocking and the rubrics review classified it as warning.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Child base inputs can merge into an invalid combination</summary>

**Cause:** The code rejects `decision.baseBranch && decision.basePr`, but later passes `decision.baseBranch || baseBranch` and `decision.basePr || basePr`.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate the effective inherited-plus-planner base inputs before creating the child issue or dispatching the child orchestrator, and fail if both are present.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Duplicate child lookup may be unreliable</summary>

**Cause:** `findExistingSubOrchestrationIssue()` searches for marker fragments like `parent:51` and `stage:stage-1`, which may be interpreted by GitHub search instead of treated as literal body text.

**Candidate solutions:** Add coverage against real or mocked `gh issue list --search` behavior, or use a lookup strategy that fetches candidate issues and validates parsed markers directly.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: unresolved branch changes are needed, and the retry-safety, marker validation, base-input validation, and duplicate-lookup coverage are concrete enough for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Make terminal child report-back retry-safe so parent resume cannot be lost after partial failure.
- [ ] Validate reused child issue markers against the requested parent, stage, and reusable state.
- [ ] Validate effective child `base_branch`/`base_pr` inputs after inherited values are merged.
- [ ] Add regression tests for dispatch failure after terminal callback state changes and mismatched child marker reuse.
- [ ] Verify or harden duplicate child lookup so marker search cannot silently create duplicate children.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Review synthesis recommends FIX_PR and identifies concrete unresolved branch changes for NEEDS_REWORK; round budget remains available.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777563256343 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNzQxNjMwMzg6cmV2aWV3OjY2OmZpeF9wcjo2Njo0 -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-51/codex-25173642474`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Made terminal child report-back retry-safe by dispatching parent resume before moving the child marker terminal, with progress-comment resume markers for idempotent reruns.
- Tightened child issue reuse: explicit reuse now requires a matching parent, normalized stage, and `running` marker; duplicate lookup now parses marker-bearing issues from a broader search.
- Validated effective child `base_branch` / `base_pr` after planner and inherited values are merged.
- Updated docs and added regression coverage for dispatch failure, mismatched reuse, effective base-input rejection, parsed duplicate lookup, and idempotent terminal reruns.
- Verification: `npm test` passed (427 tests).

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded after addressing the NEEDS_REWORK findings; a follow-up review is warranted to verify retry-safety, marker validation, effective base-input validation, duplicate lookup hardening, and new regression coverage.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777563591773 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNzQ0NTE2NTU6Zml4X3ByOjY2OnJldmlldzo2Njo1 -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 91 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Terminal child stops update a visible parent progress comment, dispatch parent resume, then mark the child terminal so failed dispatch remains retryable ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L320-L389)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Delegated `/orchestrate` dispatches still re-check route authorization ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L496-L520)), and parent resume preserves requester/access context ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L364-L381)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Planner fields are explicit (`child_stage`, `child_instructions`, `child_issue_number`, `base_branch`, `base_pr`) and runtime rejects both planner and effective merged base conflicts ([handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L142-L166), [orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L255-L262)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | partial | 6/8 | Marker parse/format/state helpers are centralized in `sub-orchestration.ts`, but issue creation/reuse logic is partly duplicated between the new standalone CLI and `orchestrate-handoff.ts`. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 5/7 | Dispatch failure after terminal callback is logged and covered by regression tests ([orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L515-L535)), but `fetchIssue`/`readPrBody` still swallow GitHub read errors into `null`/empty results ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L149-L169), [orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L292-L300)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Architecture and technical docs describe issue-backed child orchestration, reuse, terminal callback, retry behavior, and parent resume ([agent-orchestrator.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/167a91be0e5ea5d150afa14fbef56938b56f2ffe/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L88-L115)). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to orchestrator handoff, child marker helpers, one preflight input, docs/prompts, and focused tests. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | partial | 6/7 | The PR avoids lane/session-envelope threading and uses issue metadata as requested, with minor complexity from duplicate child issue helper paths. |

## Notes

- The important earlier gaps appear fixed at the current PR head: reusable child issue markers are validated for parent/stage/state, effective `base_branch`/`base_pr` conflicts are rejected, duplicate lookup parses markers, and parent resume is retry-safe.
- GitHub native parent/sub-issue linking is not implemented, but the request explicitly allowed that as a follow-up if not easy.
- I attempted `npm test` from a temporary PR archive; it failed because dependencies/type packages were not installed in that archive environment, not because of a targeted test failure.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Terminal child report-back can still silently no-op when reading the PR body or child issue fails, because `readPrBody` and `fetchIssue` catch all errors and return empty/null. This is a residual `Surface stateful failures` concern; surfacing a warning when terminal callback cannot read expected GitHub state would make retries easier to diagnose.
- **INFO:** `create-sub-orchestration-issue.ts` and `orchestrate-handoff.ts` both contain child issue creation/reuse plumbing. This is acceptable for this PR, but extracting the shared issue create/read/validate path would better satisfy `Reuse existing code`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## Notes

- The current PR head addresses the earlier rubric risks: child issue reuse validates parent/stage/state, effective base inputs are validated, duplicate lookup parses markers, requester authorization context is preserved, and terminal parent resume is retry-safe.
- GitHub native parent/sub-issue linking was requested only “if not too hard”; the PR uses visible parent comments and hidden markers instead, which is acceptable follow-up scope.
- Smallest useful follow-up: log or warn when terminal callback cannot read the expected PR body or child issue, so missing report-back state is diagnosable.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Terminal child report-back can still silently no-op when `readPrBody` or `fetchIssue` fails, because those helpers catch all errors and return empty/null. This is a residual `Surface stateful failures` issue, though the dispatch-after-progress retry path is now covered.

- **INFO:** `create-sub-orchestration-issue.ts` and `orchestrate-handoff.ts` still share some child issue creation/reuse responsibilities indirectly. This is acceptable for this PR, but extracting more of that path would improve `Reuse existing code`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #66 implements the cleaner issue-backed sub-orchestrator model requested in #51.
- A parent issue orchestrator can create or reuse a child issue, dispatch normal child `/orchestrate` work, and receive terminal child results back on the parent issue.
- The PR also preserves requester authorization through delegated dispatches, supports child `base_branch` / `base_pr`, updates docs/prompts, and adds regression coverage.
- This replaces the earlier lane-heavy approach with durable GitHub issue metadata and visible parent progress comments.

## Review
- The current design direction is sound, and the earlier blocking findings appear fixed at PR head `0b97541c`.
- Remaining concerns are terminal callback observability/idempotency edges plus one maintainability note; none appear to invalidate the main happy path.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Terminal callback read failures are silent | WARNING | `fetchIssue()` and `readPrBody()` catch all read/parse failures and return empty values, so terminal report-back can quietly skip parent resume. |
| Terminal reruns can overwrite completed child state | WARNING | `reportTerminalToParent()` accepts already-terminal child markers and still rewrites marker state from the current run result, so stale reruns can downgrade durable child state. |
| Child issue creation logic is split | INFO | The standalone `create-sub-orchestration-issue.ts` CLI is compiled but not currently called, while `orchestrate-handoff.ts` owns the active create/reuse path. |

## Progress
- Earlier blockers around parent-resume retry safety, existing child marker validation, effective base-input validation, and duplicate lookup were addressed.
- The rubrics review now passes and notes only residual read-failure observability plus shared-helper cleanup.
- I posted one new inline comment for the terminal marker overwrite risk at `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:398`.
- The read-failure warning was already clearly covered in existing top-level PR feedback, so I did not duplicate it inline.
- Native GitHub parent/sub-issue linking remains acceptable follow-up scope because #51 requested it only if easy.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Terminal callback read failures are silent</summary>

**Cause:** `fetchIssue()` returns `null` and `readPrBody()` returns `""` for all GitHub/API/JSON failures, so `resolveChildIssueForTerminal()` can return `null` and `reportTerminalToParent()` exits without triggering the outer warning path.

**Candidate solutions:** Let those reads throw during terminal callback resolution, or return an explicit error state that `reportTerminalToParent()` logs as a warning while keeping the child marker retryable.

**Comments:** All review inputs called this out as the main residual `Surface stateful failures` issue.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Terminal reruns can overwrite completed child state</summary>

**Cause:** The marker guard allows `running`, `done`, `blocked`, and `failed`, then `updateSubOrchestratorMarkerState()` writes the current terminal result even when parent resume was already dispatched.

**Candidate solutions:** Return early when a marker is already terminal and the progress comment has `resume:dispatched`; only allow terminal markers through for incomplete resume recovery.

**Comments:** This is an idempotency edge rather than a primary-flow blocker, but it can make durable child issue state disagree with the parent progress comment.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Child issue creation logic is split</summary>

**Cause:** `create-sub-orchestration-issue.ts` implements similar child issue create/reuse validation, but the active workflow path uses `ensureSubOrchestrationIssue()` inside `orchestrate-handoff.ts`.

**Candidate solutions:** Either remove the unused CLI or extract a shared helper so both paths use the same create/reuse/parent-round behavior.

**Comments:** Reviewers differed on severity here; synthesis treats it as INFO because it is maintainability cleanup, not required for correctness.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining terminal callback observability and idempotency fixes are concrete branch changes and safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Surface terminal callback GitHub read failures instead of silently returning `null` or empty PR body content.
- [ ] Prevent already-dispatched terminal child markers from being overwritten by stale terminal reruns.
- [ ] Add focused regression coverage for terminal read failures and stale terminal rerun marker handling.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis for PR #66 recommends FIX_PR for concrete MINOR_ISSUES; round budget remains available.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-51/codex-25173642474`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

Implemented the targeted PR #66 fixes.

- Terminal callback PR/issue reads now use strict read paths, so GitHub read failures surface through the existing warning instead of silently skipping parent resume.
- Already-dispatched terminal child markers now return without rewriting state, preventing stale reruns from downgrading `done` to `failed` or `blocked`.
- Added focused regression tests for PR body read failure, child issue read failure, and stale terminal rerun marker preservation.

Verification:
- `npm test` passed: 431 tests.
- `npm run build` passed after the final cleanup.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded on PR #66 and pushed targeted fixes for the prior MINOR_ISSUES; follow-up review is warranted within the round budget.

| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The PR is scoped to orchestrator handoff behavior, child marker helpers, prompt/docs, workflow authorization context inputs, and focused tests. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | partial | 6/7 | The issue-backed child model matches issue #51’s cleaner direction and avoids lane-heavy session changes, with minor remaining complexity from the split child-issue helper paths. |

## Notes

- The current PR head appears to address the earlier rubric risks: child issue reuse validates parent/stage/state, effective base inputs are validated, duplicate lookup parses markers, terminal callback failures surface, and stale terminal reruns no longer overwrite completed child state.
- Native GitHub parent/sub-issue linking is not implemented, but issue #51 explicitly framed that as optional follow-up if not easy.
- I did not run the full test suite in this pass; the PR includes focused regression tests and the latest fix-pr comment reports `npm test` and `npm run build` passed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** Child issue creation/reuse logic is still partly split between the standalone creation CLI and the active `orchestrate-handoff.ts` path. This is acceptable for this PR, but a shared helper would improve the `Reuse existing code` score.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #66 implements the cleaner issue-backed sub-orchestrator model requested in issue #51.
- A parent issue orchestrator can create or reuse a child issue, dispatch normal child `/orchestrate` work, receive terminal child results, and resume the parent planner.
- The PR also propagates requester authorization context through delegated workflows, supports child `base_branch` / `base_pr`, and updates docs/prompts/tests.
- This replaces the earlier lane-heavy approach from PR #61 with durable GitHub issue metadata and visible parent progress comments.

## Review
- Current head `af5faee78a5f98aab067951e1454df9d553921fb` resolves the earlier blocking issues; reviewers broadly consider the PR shippable.
- One reviewer flagged a narrow diagnostics warning, while Codex and rubrics review found no blocking or required branch-change issues.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Misleading sub-orchestration warning on ordinary PR read failure | WARNING | `readPrBodyStrict()` runs before confirming the terminal PR belongs to child orchestration, so a transient PR read failure can log a misleading sub-orchestration warning. |
| Child issue creation/reuse logic remains split | INFO | The active handoff path and standalone child issue CLI still share similar create/reuse responsibilities. This is maintainability cleanup, not a correctness blocker. |
| Native GitHub sub-issue links are not implemented | INFO | The PR uses visible parent progress comments plus hidden child markers instead. Issue #51 framed native parent/sub-issue linking as optional follow-up scope. |

## Progress
- Earlier blockers around retry-safe parent resume, reused child marker validation, effective base-input validation, duplicate lookup, and stale terminal reruns are fixed.
- Latest rubrics review is PASS, scoring 95 across 9 rubrics.
- Latest fix-pr comment reports `npm test` passed with 431 tests and `npm run build` passed.
- I posted one inline comment for the remaining line-specific warning at `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:322`.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Misleading sub-orchestration warning on ordinary PR read failure</summary>

**Cause:** `resolveChildIssueForTerminal()` calls `readPrBodyStrict()` for every terminal PR before it knows whether the PR closes a child orchestration issue.

**Candidate solutions:** Use a lenient first PR-body read and return `null` when no closing issue can be found; reserve strict failure surfacing for reading the linked candidate child issue.

**Comments:** Claude classified this as a WARNING. Codex and rubrics review did not treat it as required before merge.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Child issue creation/reuse logic remains split</summary>

**Cause:** `create-sub-orchestration-issue.ts` and `orchestrate-handoff.ts` both contain child issue creation/reuse plumbing, while the active production path is in `orchestrate-handoff.ts`.

**Candidate solutions:** Leave as-is for this PR, or later extract a shared helper if the standalone CLI remains useful.

**Comments:** Reviewers agree this is INFO-level cleanup.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Native GitHub sub-issue links are not implemented</summary>

**Cause:** The implementation records parent/child state through issue body markers and parent progress comments, not GitHub native parent/sub-issue relationships.

**Candidate solutions:** Treat native linking as a follow-up if GitHub API support and permissions make it straightforward.

**Comments:** This matches the issue #51 caveat: “if not too hard.”

</details>

HUMAN_DECISION: the remaining concerns are shippable diagnostics/cleanup tradeoffs, so maintainers should decide whether to merge now or request polish.

SHIP

@sepo-agent /answer
look at this https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/66#pullrequestreview-4207425242 ?

I looked at that review. The confusing bit is real: this PR uses `orchestrate` in two roles.

- As a **meta planner action**, `next_action: orchestrate` means “create/reuse a child issue, then dispatch a normal `/orchestrate` run on that child issue.”
- As a **child issue run**, `source_action: orchestrate` just starts the existing issue flow: `implement -> review -> fix-pr`.

That is why a few pieces look hacky.

For your inline comments:

- The added `author_association`, `access_policy`, and `repository_private` inputs in review/implement/fix-pr are there to preserve the **original human requester’s authorization context** across orchestrated workflow dispatches. Otherwise follow-up dispatches are made by the app/workflow actor and delegated route checks can no longer know whether the original requester was allowed. They feed back into `applyDelegatedRouteAuthorization()` here: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/af5faee78a5f98aab067951e1454df9d553921fb/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L520-L536

- The preflight condition here is intentional but deserves a comment: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/af5faee78a5f98aab067951e1454df9d553921fb/.agent/src/cli/orchestrator-preflight.ts#L18-L20  
  It says: only run the LLM planner for `source_action=orchestrate` when the target is an **issue**. That is the parent/meta issue planner case. PR `/orchestrate` stays deterministic and goes to review/fix-pr behavior.

- `normalizeAgentAction()` is not useless now: `parsePlannerDecision()` uses it to accept planner JSON with `next_action: orchestrate`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/af5faee78a5f98aab067951e1454df9d553921fb/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L110-L142  
  Then `decideAgentHandoff()` only allows that when the current source is the issue-level meta orchestrator: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/af5faee78a5f98aab067951e1454df9d553921fb/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L344-L389

High-level structure of `orchestrate-handoff.ts`:

1. Decide next action from planner/manual/heuristic state.
2. If terminal, try `reportTerminalToParent()` so child issue/PR results get reported back to the parent issue.
3. If `nextAction === "orchestrate"`, call `ensureSubOrchestrationIssue()` to reuse/create the child issue.
4. Write a dedupe marker.
5. Dispatch `agent-review.yml`, `agent-implement.yml`, `agent-fix-pr.yml`, or `agent-orchestrator.yml`.

I think the implementation is functionally aligned with #51, but the naming is confusing. Best cleanup would be to rename the planner-level meaning from generic `orchestrate` to something like `child_orchestrate` or add a small typed wrapper/comment around that branch. I would treat that as readability cleanup, not a correctness blocker.

@sepo-agent /answer 

- Given the nature of this implementation, it's effectively a different route other than the orchestrate route right? (as we do not support hierarchical orchestrating for now -> which might be interesting to explore later actually). In that case, what could be a better name for that? Or should we not do it? 

 - Also I don't think the author association is not necessary -> I think the right way to implement it is simply check the access of the orchestrate route at the beginning -> (making sure it should have access to all these routes) if not enough access the orchestrate will simply be rejected. 
 
 - Further more, I think the implementation is not clean -> please reflect on what's the right way to design the orchestrator, and what's the right way to address the issues (like session continuation, tracking etc)? 

Let's think more about the design here.

I think you are right to push on the design. The current PR is solving the right product problem, but it is muddy because it overloads `orchestrate` as both a user route and an internal planner action.

My preferred framing:

- **`orchestrate` remains the public user route**: “coordinate work on this issue/PR.”
- The parent orchestrator should emit an **internal command**, not a new public route.
- That internal command should be named something like `delegate_issue`, `start_child_issue`, or `spawn_child_task`.
- The child issue then runs the normal existing `/orchestrate` flow.

So I would avoid adding `orchestrate` to `AgentAction`. `AgentAction` currently means concrete follow-up routes like `implement`, `review`, `fix-pr` in [`handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L3). A child-issue delegation is a different kind of decision. It deserves a separate type, roughly:

```ts
type OrchestratorDecision =
  | { kind: "dispatch"; route: "implement" | "review" | "fix-pr"; targetNumber: string }
  | { kind: "delegate_issue"; stage: string; instructions: string; childIssueNumber?: string; baseBranch?: string; basePr?: string }
  | { kind: "stop"; reason: string }
  | { kind: "blocked"; reason: string };
```

On access: I agree with your instinct. Passing `author_association` through every child workflow is plumbing we should avoid if we can. The cleaner rule is:

1. At the initial `/orchestrate` request, check whether the requester is allowed to use `orchestrate`.
2. If `orchestrate` can indirectly run `implement`, `review`, and `fix-pr`, then either:
   - require `orchestrate` access to imply access to all delegated routes, or
   - at orchestrator start, check the requester against the full capability set: `orchestrate + implement + review + fix-pr`.
3. If not sufficient, reject the orchestrate request up front.

That makes `orchestrate` a privileged coordinator route. Internal child dispatches then do not need to re-prove the requester’s association on every hop. We can still carry `requested_by` for audit/display, but not use downstream workflow inputs as the authorization mechanism.

The larger design principle should be: **separate route authorization, orchestration state, and session continuity**.

- **Authorization**: checked once at the user boundary, against the orchestration capability set.
- **Orchestration state**: stored in GitHub-visible state: parent issue status comment, child issue marker, child terminal report marker.
- **Session continuity**: best-effort LLM continuity for the same target/route/lane only. It should not be the source of truth for parent/child workflow state.

The right architecture for this feature is probably:

```text
Parent issue #N
  meta orchestrator session: issue:N/orchestrator/planner

  -> internal command: delegate_issue
       creates/reuses child issue #C
       child issue marker: parent #N, stage, state running

  -> child issue #C
       normal orchestrate flow
       implement -> review -> fix-pr -> terminal

  -> terminal callback
       resolves child issue from issue/PR
       posts/updates parent progress comment
       marks child done/blocked/failed
       resumes parent orchestrator
```

For tracking/session continuation, I would not solve same-issue hierarchical sessions yet. Use child issues as the boundary. That means every child has its own natural thread key because the target issue number differs. Later, if we want true hierarchical orchestrating inside the same issue, then we can add explicit lanes/chain IDs. But that is a separate design.

So my recommendation: keep the idea, but refactor the model before merging. Rename the planner action away from `orchestrate`, remove `orchestrate` from `AgentAction`, make child delegation its own internal decision type, and move authorization to an up-front orchestration capability check.

> I think you are right to push on the design. The current PR is solving the right product problem, but it is muddy because it overloads `orchestrate` as both a user route and an internal planner action.
> 
> My preferred framing:
> 
> - **`orchestrate` remains the public user route**: “coordinate work on this issue/PR.”
> - The parent orchestrator should emit an **internal command**, not a new public route.
> - That internal command should be named something like `delegate_issue`, `start_child_issue`, or `spawn_child_task`.
> - The child issue then runs the normal existing `/orchestrate` flow.
> 
> So I would avoid adding `orchestrate` to `AgentAction`. `AgentAction` currently means concrete follow-up routes like `implement`, `review`, `fix-pr` in [`handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L3). A child-issue delegation is a different kind of decision. It deserves a separate type, roughly:
> 
> ```ts

>   | { kind: "dispatch"; route: "implement" | "review" | "fix-pr"; targetNumber: string }
>   | { kind: "delegate_issue"; stage: string; instructions: string; childIssueNumber?: string; baseBranch?: string; basePr?: string }
>   | { kind: "stop"; reason: string }
>   | { kind: "blocked"; reason: string };
> ```
> 
> On access: I agree with your instinct. Passing `author_association` through every child workflow is plumbing we should avoid if we can. The cleaner rule is:
> 
> 1. At the initial `/orchestrate` request, check whether the requester is allowed to use `orchestrate`.
> 2. If `orchestrate` can indirectly run `implement`, `review`, and `fix-pr`, then either:
>    - require `orchestrate` access to imply access to all delegated routes, or
>    - at orchestrator start, check the requester against the full capability set: `orchestrate + implement + review + fix-pr`.
> 3. If not sufficient, reject the orchestrate request up front.
> 
> That makes `orchestrate` a privileged coordinator route. Internal child dispatches then do not need to re-prove the requester’s association on every hop. We can still carry `requested_by` for audit/display, but not use downstream workflow inputs as the authorization mechanism.
> 
> The larger design principle should be: **separate route authorization, orchestration state, and session continuity**.
> 
> - **Authorization**: checked once at the user boundary, against the orchestration capability set.
> - **Orchestration state**: stored in GitHub-visible state: parent issue status comment, child issue marker, child terminal report marker.
> - **Session continuity**: best-effort LLM continuity for the same target/route/lane only. It should not be the source of truth for parent/child workflow state.
> 
> The right architecture for this feature is probably:
> 
> ```text
> Parent issue #N
>   meta orchestrator session: issue:N/orchestrator/planner
> 
>   -> internal command: delegate_issue
>        creates/reuses child issue #C
>        child issue marker: parent #N, stage, state running
> 
>   -> child issue #C
>        normal orchestrate flow
>        implement -> review -> fix-pr -> terminal
> 
>   -> terminal callback
>        resolves child issue from issue/PR
>        posts/updates parent progress comment
>        marks child done/blocked/failed
>        resumes parent orchestrator
> ```
> 
> For tracking/session continuation, I would not solve same-issue hierarchical sessions yet. Use child issues as the boundary. That means every child has its own natural thread key because the target issue number differs. Later, if we want true hierarchical orchestrating inside the same issue, then we can add explicit lanes/chain IDs. But that is a separate design.
> 
> So my recommendation: keep the idea, but refactor the model before merging. Rename the planner action away from `orchestrate`, remove `orchestrate` from `AgentAction`, make child delegation its own internal decision type, and move authorization to an up-front orchestration capability check.

title: Support child issue sub-orchestrators
